title: Monteverdia loeseneri
taxonomy: Monteverdia loeseneri is classified within the family Celastraceae. This family consists of about 90 to 100 genera and is commonly known as the bittersweet family. The genus Monteverdia, to which M. loeseneri belongs, is a group of flowering plants primarily found in the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. Monteverdia loeseneri is native to Central and South America and has been identified in countries such as Costa Rica and Panama. Its classification is also supported by its characteristic morphology, including its simple, alternate leaves and small, white flowers.
